violations.text: Soap was not available at a hand-washing sink in the upstairs kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Soap was provided at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

Raw animal foods were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods in the various refrigeration. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Foods were rearranged for proper storage at the time of inspection Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwashers at the upstairs bars was less than 50 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Per the PIC, glasses are to be washed/rinsed/sanitized at kitchen dish machine until units adjusted/repaired. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

Several items held in the first-floor kitchen hot hold unit were observed not properly reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit prior to hot holding. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. Food items were properly reheated at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

Several items prepped on site were observed held beyond seven days from the date of preparation marked on the item... - Garlic Aioli 7-29-25 - Dill Aioli 8-5-25 - Caesar Dressing 8-11-25 - House Dressing 7-9-25 Dressings were voluntarily disposed of by the PIC at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

Working spray bottle containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Name of chemicals were added at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

Frozen Reduced Oxygen Packaged Lobster was observed thawing in standing water in the food prep sink. ROP Lobster was observed at 32 degrees Fahrenheit and removed from packaging at the time of inspection. Proper thawing was reviewed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025

The following equipment was observed to be in need of repair/adjustment... - Tubing to the dish machine at the outdoor rooftop deck was observed to be broken/cracked not allowing for proper sanitizing during the rinse cycle. Tubing is to be replaced. - The pressure gauge to the first-floor kitchen dish washer was observed improperly working during the rinse cycle (Shifting to below 0 psi when rinse cycle began). Pressure gauge is to read 15 to 25 psi. - The cold water to the upstairs kitchen hand sink was observed to be turned off (per PIC due to a leak). Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/02/2025
Pasta and calamari dressing were observed held past their seven day date marking. PIC voluntarily disposed of food at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2024

The floor in the second floor kitchen area had an accumulation of old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/08/2024

At the time of inspection, Sauces/Dressings prepared in the food establishment were observed in the outdoor walk-in cooler held beyond 7 days of the preparation date marked on the containers. TIME/TEMPERATURE CONTROL FOR SAFETY FOOD prepared and held in a FOOD ESTABLISHMENT for more than 24 hours shall be clearly marked to indicate the date or day by which the FOOD shall be consumed on the PREMISES, sold, or discarded when held at a temperature of 5ºC (41ºF) or less for a maximum of 7 days. The day of preparation shall be counted as Day 1. Food items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. (See disposal) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of the PIC, Risotto was placed hot in the refrigerator in a covered container greater than four inches deep (Observed at 60 degrees Fahrenheit). Based on time of preparation and temperature, Risotto was spread out in shallow pans to ensure proper cooling at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2023

Employees were observed eating in the dry storage area. Employees moved to separate location within the restaurant at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

An employees' open beverage container was observed stored in a small flip top prep unit. Employees beverage containers shall be tightly covered and placed in a protected location to prevent contamination of foods and food items. Cup was disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

The hand-washing sink in the upstairs kitchen was blocked by a fan and laundry basket. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. (The Fan and Laundry Basket was moved at the time of inspection.) Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (deli slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Deli slicer was set for proper wash/rinse/sanitize at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

At the time of inspections, several food items (Cheese Sauce, Baked Potatoes, and Risotto) which were prepared previous day, were not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Food items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for the following time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods... - Per interview of the PIC, several food items (Baked Potatoes, Sliced Potatoes, Risotto, Cheese Sauce, Rice w/ Chourico) were placed warm in refrigeration units in covered containers greater than four inches deep with tight fitting lids. - Per the PIC, Clams and Mozzarella Cheese were recently prepped and not allowed to properly cool prior to being placed in the prep unit(s). Food items were moved to shallow containers and placed in colder refrigeration for proper cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

The presence of drain flies (Small flying insects) is not controlled as evidenced by an abundance of small flying insects observed in several areas of the establishment. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. PIC to ensure that pest control increases frequency of service and to address conditions leading to flying insect pest issues. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

The following In-Use Utensils were observed improperly stored between uses... - Knives were observed being stored between prep unit surfaces. - A knife and an ice cream scoop were observed being stored in stagnant water. Utensils were moved to dish area at the time of insepction. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location (in a pan). Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

At the time of inspection, the exterior Walk-in Cooler was observed not in good repair. The cooler fan unit was observed to be covered with ice and maintaining an ambient temperature of 45 to 49 degrees Fahrenheit. As observed during inspection, a temporary walk-in cooler unit was obtained and foods were in in the process of being relocated to the colder walk-in unit. New Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023
The facility is lacking thermo-labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine at the high temperature dish machine. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023

The drain lines for the Island Oasis mixer machines were observed to be draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. Both Island Oasis units were moved to proper drainage locations at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/26/2023
